title: SOP-R101-04 Reactor Cooling Deviation — Revision 4
content: Synthetic training document. Effective 2025-10-01. Revision 4 supersedes Revision 3. For a reactor cooling deviation, verify alarm validity, compare controller command with independent valve position feedback, confirm cooling-water flow and utility availability, and notify the shift supervisor. Follow the approved plant procedure and field verification process. Do not bypass interlocks or change a setpoint based on this demo document.

title: P&ID LOOP1 Area 12 — Revision 7
content: Synthetic P&ID index. R-101 cooling jacket is supplied through FV-101. Command signal FIC-101-OUT and position feedback FV-101-POS are independent Tags. Cooling flow FT-101 is downstream of FV-101. Revision 7 adds the independent position transmitter reference.

title: FV-101 Control Valve Maintenance Manual — Revision 2
content: Synthetic maintenance guidance. Position-command mismatch with reduced downstream flow can indicate stiction, actuator supply restriction, linkage wear, or feedback calibration error. A qualified technician must inspect and test the valve under the approved isolation and permit process.

title: MOC-2025-014 FV-101 Position Feedback Upgrade
content: Synthetic MOC record. Added independent valve position feedback and updated P&ID Revision 7. No controller tuning or process safety limit was changed. Commissioned 2025-09-15.

title: ePTW-2025-221 Cooling Header Inspection
content: Synthetic closed permit. Cooling header inspection completed 2025-12-11. No active isolation remains. This record is historical context and is not an authorization to perform work.

title: LOOP-1 Night Shift Handover 2025-12-31
content: Synthetic handover. Operator observed a brief FV-101 position hesitation during a routine throughput change. No process alarm occurred. Maintenance notification MN-8841 was opened for review.

title: LOOP-1 Alarm Philosophy — Revision 5
content: Synthetic alarm philosophy. Alarm floods are analyzed as related event groups. A primary equipment deviation and its dependent process alarms should not be presented as independent root causes. Safety-critical alarms remain visible and are never suppressed by the Agent.

title: FV-101 Maintenance History 2025
content: Synthetic history. WO-7702 replaced packing in March. WO-8128 calibrated the position transmitter in September. MN-8841 reports intermittent hesitation and remains planned, not completed.
